prevalence.value: 0.5-1.0% (higher in adolescent girls and young women)
prevalence.source: European eating disorder epidemiology syntheses / WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing in some countries

burden.mortalityRate: Elevated vs general population (medical complications and suicide)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 criteria with medical assessment; structured eating disorder services common in many countries

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; earlier detection via school and primary care varies by country
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: hundreds of thousands to >1 million across EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate with multidisciplinary treatment access; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist eating disorder services and structured referral pathways in many member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Multidisciplinary care (medical, nutrition, psychotherapy) with family-based therapy for adolescents; inpatient stabilization for severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Wait times for specialist services in some countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established multidisciplinary eating disorder programs in many health systems
